title: Events, Refs, SHAs, Forks, and Workflow Trust Boundaries · Delivery Incident Triage
summary: A release responder inherits a plausible but defective event and revision trust policy; preserve run evidence, isolate the first causal fault in events, refs, shas, forks, and workflow trust boundaries, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in event and revision trust policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive workflow source, checked-out revision, GITHUB_REF, GITHUB_SHA, actor, permissions, and payload from each trigger.
Choose pull request merge, head, or base revision intentionally and state what integration or source claim each can prove.
Model fork approvals, withheld secrets, read-only tokens, compute abuse, contributor changes, and re-run authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Ci Event Payload Ref Sha
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug maintainer runs fork code through pull_request_target with write authority, then a privileged workflow_run consumes its artifact. Establish workflow source, tested revision, actor, permissions, producer trust, and data-only inspection. Competency probe: Derive workflow source, checked-out revision, GITHUB_REF, GITHUB_SHA, actor, permissions, and payload from each trigger.

Inspect the Ci Event Payload Ref Sha trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Ci Event Payload Ref Sha.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Ci Event Payload Ref Sha, keep Ci Event Payload Ref Sha connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

Establish workflow source, tested revision, actor, permissions, producer trust, and data-only inspection. stimulus.title: Ci Event Payload Ref Sha case record

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A debug maintainer runs fork code through pull_request_target with write authority, then a privileged workflow_run consumes its artifact. options.text: For inspect the failure record, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Ci Event Payload Ref Sha, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-workflow-run-artifact-trust-wxn8b0". Tailor its event-trust-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, keep Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-workflow-run-artifact-trust-wxn8b0
  - competency: ci-workflow-run-artifact-trust
    artifact: event-trust-policy.yaml
    decision: workflow run artifact trust
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: change the event from pull_request to pull_request_target
    retained-proof: job result dependency graph exact command and first causal failure
    case-key: wxn8b0
    change: |
      event: pull_request
      workflow-source: merge-commit
      checkout-revision: pull-request-merge-sha
      token: read-only
      secrets: withheld
      untrusted-code: isolated-execution
      privileged-followup: data-only
      producer-proof: run-id-and-workflow-sha
    verify: compare pull_request pull_request_target workflow_run refs SHAs tokens secrets and checked-out code
    failure: Reject fork code under pull_request_target secrets, maintainer rerun as trust, or successful artifacts as executable proof.
    repair: Use low-privilege pull_request execution, separate privileged follow-up, and validate producer and content strictly as data.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug maintainer runs fork code through pull_request_target with write authority, then a privileged workflow_run consumes its artifact. Establish workflow source, tested revision, actor, permissions, producer trust, and data-only inspection. Competency probe: Treat workflow_run, issue_comment, repository_dispatch, and downloaded artifacts according to producer identity, revision, permissions, and content trust.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: An artifact from a successful low-privilege workflow is safe to execute in a privileged workflow.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: An artifact from a successful low-privilege workflow is safe to execute in a privileged workflow.
